what_it_does: This file is a systemd user service unit that launches the Qwen3-TTS voice engine Python script on port 4128 from a dedicated virtualenv, keeps it running with a restart-on-failure policy, and sends output to the system journal.
observations: Type=simple service with a 10-second restart delay
ExecStart uses %h/.voice-venvs/qwen3tts/bin/python to run /home/tim/company/voice/engines/qwen3tts.py with argument 4128
A comment states the model is already in the HF cache so startup cost is only GPU load
Description labels the service as a TRIAL STARTER
standouts: Qwen3-TTS engine running under a dedicated .voice-venvs/qwen3tts virtualenv
Explicit note that startup is GPU-load-only because the model is cached
Marked as a trial starter deployment
conventions: Follows systemd unit file INI section structure
Uses %h for user home directory in some paths while mixing absolute /home/tim paths in others
Externalizes environment variables into a separate .env file
Uses journal logging and restart-on-failure for a long-running service
concerns: Mixes %h expansion with absolute /home/tim paths in the same unit
Port 4128 is hardcoded on the command line rather than parameterized
No explicit User= or Group= directive, so behavior depends on how the unit is installed
After=default.target only; no explicit network or sound subsystem dependency
GPU dependency is mentioned only in a comment with no resource or device constraints
notes: A user-level systemd wrapper that keeps the Qwen3-TTS voice inference endpoint alive on port 4128 with externalized environment config.
questions: What variables are expected in voice.env?
What interface or protocol does the engine expose on port 4128?
Is this intended to run as a user unit or a system unit?
Why is port 4128 chosen and is it configurable?
What does TRIAL STARTER imply about lifecycle or licensing?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches: the Description says it runs Qwen3-TTS VoiceDesign on port 4128, and ExecStart does exactly that by invoking qwen3tts.py with port 4128.
intention_signals: The file is trying to turn the Qwen3-TTS engine into a reliable, auto-starting background service that is easy to configure via an environment file and monitor through the journal.
novelty: The Description explicitly tags the service as a TRIAL STARTER and includes a comment clarifying that the model is already cached, so startup time is dominated by GPU loading rather than download.
contribution: Provides the operational runtime contract for the Qwen3-TTS engine: how it is started, under which environment, how it restarts, and how its output is logged, which a fused system would keep to preserve deployment behavior.
summary_for_embedding: A systemd user service unit that launches the Qwen3-TTS voice engine from a dedicated virtualenv on port 4128 with restart-on-failure and journal logging.
intention_for_embedding: To keep a local Qwen3-TTS voice inference endpoint persistently available as a user-session service.
